text: A standard representation of common criteria of qualitative selection and exclusion shall be defined according to Articles 45-50 of Directive 2004/18/EC.
The right granularity and consistency of criteria shall be found through testing and iterative re-definition in order to ensure legitimacy and to retrieve adequate mapping results.
National requirements for qualitative selection and exclusion shall correspond to the EC criteria given in Article 45-50.
A template that ensures comparable and reliable mappings between criteria for qualitative selection and exclusion and national attestations shall be defined.
The members of this work package shall perform the mapping between national attestations and European criteria of qualitative selection and exclusion.
Legitimacy of standard representation of common criteria of qualitative selection and exclusion shall be ensured.
Legitimacy of standard mapping template shall be ensured.
Legitimacy of mapping between national attestations and common criteria for qualitative selection and exclusion shall be ensured.
A decision to deploy either a centralised or a decentralized infrastructure that provides the mapping service shall be made.
Technical means to provide the mapping service shall be defined.
An entity that hosts the mapping facility shall be defined in dependence of the type of infrastructure and the dedicated governing model.
A governing model with the necessary status and power shall be formally defined in order to ensure fulfilment of functions like liability, sustainability and maintainability.
Liability of organisation that governs the infrastructure and mapping facility shall be defined.
The mapping facility shall ensure sustainability of the system over time based on commonly agreed mapping schemes.
A formal procedure to make changes to the system shall be defined.
The mapping facility shall be accessible to all relevant key stakeholders such as economic operators, contracting authorities, certifiers and national service providers.
Identity and access management shall provide a functionality to register an economic operator in a formal procedure in order to create a VCD account.
Identity and access management shall provide an interface to submit sensitive economic operator data in order to allow the economic operator to apply for the creation of a VCD account.
Sensitive economic operator data shall be verified through a formal procedure by an officer.
Sensitive economic operator data shall be placed to a VCD account by an officer.
Identity and access management shall provide a functionality that enables the economic operator to receive his log-in data.
A change procedure shall be defined in order to modify sensitive economic operator data that is connected to the VCD account.
The economic operator shall be able to delete his account.
The VCD system shall provide a functionality to check through identity and access management whether an economic operator has sufficient user rights to access his account.
The VCD system shall provide an interface where the economic operator can provide non-sensitive economic operator data.
The VCD system shall provide an interface where tender-specific data can be either fetched from or be provided through.
The VCD system shall provide an interface where the economic operator can request the creation of a VCD package.
The VCD system shall have an interface to receive and process the information provided by the pre-VCD mapping tool.
The VCD system shall provide a functionality that suggests a possible compilation of the VCD to the economic operator according to the tender-specific criteria.
The VCD system shall provide a functionality where the economic operator can decide which particular attestation services should be invoked and collected.
The VCD system shall provide a functionality that displays the attestation services that cannot be invoked automatically and that have to be manually added by the economic operator.
The VCD system shall have an interface/technical access to retrieve attestations that can be automatically retrieved from issuing bodies.
The VCD system shall have a functionality to extract structural data of evidence from attestations that can be retrieved automatically.
The VCD system shall have an interface to submit/upload evidence that have to be provided manually by the economic operator.
The evidence submitted to the VCD system shall correspond to the mapping information suggested by the VCD system.
The VCD system shall provide an interface where an economic operator can add structural data of evidence that belong to specific evidence.
The VCD system shall provide an interface where the economic operator can initiate the compilation process.
The VCD system shall provide a functionality to compile various data formats from various sources into a single VCD package.
The VCD package shall provide a functionality to add structural VCD package data to the VCD package and VCD system during the compilation process.
The VCD package shall provide a functionality to add tender-specific data to the VCD package and VCD system during the compilation process.
The VCD package shall provide a functionality to add economic operator data to the VCD package and VCD system during the compilation process.
The VCD package shall provide a functionality to add structural data of evidence to the VCD package and VCD system during the compilation process.
The VCD package shall provide a functionality to add evidences to the VCD package and VCD system during the compilation process.
The VCD package shall provide a functionality to add translations to the VCD package and VCD system during the compilation process.
The VCD system shall provide a functionality to notify the economic operator when the compilation process is finished.
The VCD system shall provide interfaces to deliver the VCD to the economic operator.
VCD system shall be operated by at least one national VCD service provider.
The VCD system shall have the status of a trusted system.
Direct access to attestations issued by issuing bodies shall be established through organisational agreements.
The compilation suggestion that is given to the economic operator by the VCD system shall be reliable with regard to fulfilment of tender-specific requirements.
User rights for the VCD system shall be defined.
Structural data of evidence shall be defined.
The evidence submitted to the VCD system shall be eligible for the requirements mentioned by §§45-46 of European Directive 2004/18/EC.

The VCD transportation system shall provide a functionality that enables both parties involved, contracting authorities and economic operators, to authenticate to the VCD transportation system before using it.
The VCD transportation system shall provide a functionality that enables the economic operator to find an electronic address of a contracting authority for receiving VCD packages.
The VCD transportation system shall have an interface to connect to it in order to submit the VCD package to a contracting authority.
The VCD transportation system shall provide a functionality that supports the economic operator in submitting the VCD package to a contracting authority.
The transportation system shall provide an interface that enables contracting authorities to receive a VCD.
Both parties involved, contracting authorities and economic operators, shall have an electronic address for receiving/sending VCD packages.
The transport of a VCD package shall be initiated by the economic operator.
The choice of how to submit the VCD package to the contracting authority shall be the one that is preferred by the economic operator.
The transportation system shall ensure that the VCD package is being delivered to the contracting authority.
The transport of a VCD package shall be confidential.
The transport of a VCD package shall be reliable.
The transport of a VCD package shall be secure.
The transport of a VCD package shall ensure that the package has not been altered or modified during transportation or validation.
Non-repudiation of origin of a VCD package shall be supported.
The VCD viewing system shall provide a functionality to authenticate contracting authorities to view the data contained within a VCD package.
The VCD viewing system shall provide an interface that adequately displays various types of data formats contained within a single VCD package.
The VCD viewing system shall display structural VCD package data contained in the VCD package.
The VCD viewing system shall display tender-specific data contained in the VCD package.
The VCD viewing system shall display economic operator data contained in the VCD package.
The VCD viewing system shall display structural data of evidence contained in the VCD package.
The VCD viewing system shall display evidences contained in the VCD package.
The VCD viewing system shall display translations of evidences contained in the VCD package.
Any standard representation of the different types of data shall be agreed upon the pilot partners.
A VCD system shall provide functionality to collect and process context specific data for evidence that has to be provided manually or semi-automatically.
A VCD system shall provide functionality to collect and process context specific data for evidence that has to be provided automatically by the issuing body.
A VCD system shall provide IT-interfaces to issuing bodies which provide evidences in machine interpretable format.
A VCD system shall interact with the issuing bodies which provide evidences in machine interpretable format through IT-interfaces.
A VCD system shall provide a user interface to the issuing body to provide context specific data for an evidence in a structured form.
The VCD system shall provide a functionality to validate the provided context specific data during the submission process.
Interfaces shall be defined to retrieve context specific data from issuing bodies either automatically or semi-automatically.
The rule set derived from legal requirements concerning evidence checking shall be defined.
The rule set allowing for a Stage 3 mapping among concepts expressed by the context specific data shall be defined.
The rule set derived from legal requirements concerning subcontracting and other consortium constellations shall be defined.
Any VCD advanced package shall follow a common standard schema of context specific data.
Context specific data shall be machine interpretable.
Evidences that have a simple structure and limited variety of contents shall be included into the common standard schema first.
A semantic definition of each element/class of the common standard schema of context specific data shall be created.
A semantic definition of each element/class of the common standard schema of context specific data shall ensure that these data is mutually recognized.
A method and agreement procedure to develop a common standard schema of context specific data shall be defined.
Any common standard schema of context specific data classes shall be agreed upon the pilot partners.
Every pilot partner shall follow the common standard schema of context specific data by defining a national version of it.
A VCD system shall provide a user-interface to the economic operator to initiate the creation of a VCD advanced package.
A VCD system shall provide a user-interface to the economic operator to provide existing VCD packages of other economic operators.
A VCD system shall provide a functionality to check whether the structural VCD package data of existing VCD packages are consistent with the VCD advanced package schema.
A VCD system shall provide a functionality to check whether the tender specific data of existing VCD packages are consistent with other VCD packages.
A VCD system shall provide functionality to merge data from several existing VCD packages of other economic operators.
A VCD system shall provide functionality to compile the data which result from the evidence collection process.
A VCD system shall provide a user-interface to the economic operator to initiate the re-compilation of an existing VCD package.
A VCD system shall provide a user-interface to the economic operator for uploading an existing VCD package.
A VCD system shall provide a functionality to decompose an existing VCD package.
A VCD system shall provide a functionality to identify outdated or obsolete evidences.
The VCD system shall provide functionality and an interface to the economic operator that enables him to mandate a certain contracting authority to request attestations.
The VCD system shall detect which attestations can be used by the mandate feature.
The VCD system shall provide a functionality that enables National VCD Service Providers to store attestations.
A functionality for referencing to attestations shall be implemented within the VCD system.
The VCD system shall ensure that a contracting authority is able to request attestations via the provided reference.
The functionality for referencing to attestations within the VCD shall be implemented either VCD-specific or contracting authority-specific.
VCD-specific or contracting authority-specific reference to attestations shall be stored within the VCD system.
The VCD system shall provide a functionally to check whether a contracting authority has sufficient rights and has been mandated to retrieve attestations.
The VCD system shall provide a functionality and an interface that enables contracting authorities to retrieve the attestation from the storage.
The VCD system shall ensure that the attestation that is requested by the contracting authority is still valid at the time of awarding.
The VCD system shall provide a functionality that initiates a conflict resolution process between economic operator and contracting authority.
There shall be an access policy issued by the economic operator that mandates a contracting authority to request attestation on demand.
A general reference structure and syntax shall be defined.
National and local political commitments and service-level agreements shall ensure that consensuses on roles and responsibilities are built.
There shall be a service-level agreement between the parties involved about the response time of a delivery of attestations and data availability.
There shall be a service-level agreement between the parties involved about conflict resolution.
Legal systems shall ensure that context specific data and validation values have an equivalent value to conventional attestations.
The national procurement law shall ensure that National Service Provider can act as national validation points.
Liability issues of the National Service Providers shall be clarified when they act as national validation points.
National procedures with regard to Article 52 (1) of Directive 2004/18/EC shall be mutually recognized and trusted across borders.
